name: Community Action Committee Of Lehigh Valley, Inc.
description_short: Non-profit community action agency offering HUD-certified housing counseling, financial education, and advocacy services to improve economic opportunity in the Lehigh Valley.
description_long:
Community Action Committee of Lehigh Valley is a non-profit organization dedicated to improving quality of life across the Lehigh Valley region by building communities where all people have access to economic opportunity and a voice in decisions affecting their lives. The organization operates under a mission-driven model with deep roots in community advocacy and development. Their work spans multiple interconnected program areas rather than focusing on a single financial product or service.

The organization offers a comprehensive suite of community development services including HUD-certified housing counseling (pre-purchase counseling, mortgage foreclosure diversion, financial education), the Sixth Street Shelter for families experiencing homelessness, community home weatherization and renovation programs, the Rising Tide Community Loan Fund for small business development, food access initiatives through Second Harvest Food Bank partnerships, and neighborhood revitalization work. Their 2024-2025 impact statistics demonstrate significant reach: 97 families served at their shelter, 98 homes weatherized, 124,000 people monthly food access support, and $14 million in small business loans disbursed.

What distinguishes Community Action LV is their integrated approach to economic mobility that combines emergency shelter, housing stability, business lending, and financial counseling rather than offering standalone financial products. They employ HUD-certified housing counselors and maintain partnerships with local municipalities and organizations like Second Harvest Food Bank. The organization demonstrates transparency through detailed impact reporting and features real client success stories (Chelsie's path from homelessness to business ownership, Amoury's foreclosure diversion).

The primary caveat is that this organization primarily serves the Lehigh Valley region geographically. Additionally, they function as a community development resource hub rather than a direct lender or credit repair service—their Rising Tide loan fund and housing counseling require qualification and application processes. Services like foreclosure diversion and housing counseling have specific eligibility requirements tied to financial hardship circumstances.
pros: HUD-certified housing counselors providing pre-purchase and foreclosure diversion counseling at no cost to qualifying families
Rising Tide Community Loan Fund has disbursed $14 million in small business loans to underserved entrepreneurs
Integrated support ecosystem combining emergency shelter, housing counseling, and financial education in one organization
Sixth Street Shelter provides comprehensive family support including childcare coordination and job placement assistance
Weatherization program covers comprehensive energy audits, window/door repair, and high-efficiency appliance replacement
Multilingual services available in Arabic, Chinese, Portuguese, and Spanish
Demonstrates measurable community impact with transparent annual statistics and documented client success stories
cons: Services are geographically limited to Lehigh Valley and surrounding areas only
Housing counseling and loan programs require application/qualification process with specific eligibility criteria
Does not offer quick cash loans, payday alternatives, or same-day funding products
Limited information on website regarding specific APR rates or loan terms for Rising Tide Community Loan Fund
As a non-profit community agency rather than financial services company, some services may have waitlists or limited availability
best_for: Homeowners in Lehigh Valley facing foreclosure or mortgage default seeking HUD-certified counseling
First-time homebuyers in the region needing pre-purchase financial education and counseling
Low-to-moderate income small business owners in Lehigh Valley seeking affordable business financing
Families experiencing homelessness seeking emergency shelter with integrated path to housing stability and employment
diagnosis: Community Action LV is best for Lehigh Valley residents facing housing instability, foreclosure risk, or seeking small business financing who qualify for their programs. The main caveat is geographic limitation to the Lehigh Valley region and the requirement to apply and qualify for most services, which means they function as a community development resource hub rather than an immediate financial solution provider.
services: HUD-certified housing counseling (pre-purchase, post-purchase, foreclosure diversion)
Mortgage foreclosure prevention and diversion services
Financial education and financial literacy counseling
Sixth Street Shelter emergency family shelter with childcare and employment support
Rising Tide Community Loan Fund small business lending
Home weatherization program with energy audits and improvements
Community home acquisition, renovation, and resale to qualified buyers
Housing Counseling integrated with Lehigh Valley Community Land Trust services
Food access coordination through Second Harvest Food Bank partnerships
Business start-up and development support and advocacy
Neighborhood revitalization and property improvement initiatives
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
